derived_from: tools/benchmark/ground-truth/ledgerkit-defects.sealed.json, defect D-003 (running_totals_by_category assumes a fixed category set)
content_sha256_note: sha256 over canonical JSON of {task_id, class, items} only (sorted keys, no whitespace) -- the hash that must not change once this checklist is frozen; recompute and compare before trusting any copy of this file.

items.description: Posting an entry to an account whose category is not "food", "rent", or "utilities" and then calling running_totals_by_category no longer raises KeyError.
items.how_to_detect: Run the bug report's repro (post to an account with category "travel", then call running_totals_by_category) and confirm it returns normally instead of raising.

items.description: The new category's running totals are correct: the returned series for that category reflects the correct cumulative balance after each touching entry, in posting order -- same semantics as the three previously-hardcoded categories.
items.how_to_detect: Post two or more entries touching the new category across separate transactions and confirm the returned series is the correct running cumulative sequence, not just present-and-non-crashing.

items.description: Categories present in the chart but never touched by any transaction still appear in the returned dict with an empty list -- the existing test_untouched_categories_stay_empty contract is preserved, not silently dropped by switching to an only-report-what-was-touched scheme.
items.how_to_detect: Re-run (or re-derive) test_untouched_categories_stay_empty: build a chart with an untouched categorized account, post only to a different category, and confirm the untouched category's key is present with value [].

items.description: The fix derives known categories from the chart passed to the function at call time (or an equivalent non-hardcoded mechanism), not from a widened-but-still-fixed hardcoded tuple that could recur as the same class of bug later.
items.how_to_detect: Read running_totals_by_category's new implementation: confirm it no longer keys off a fixed literal tuple of category names, but instead derives the category set from ledger.chart (or uses a defaultdict/setdefault approach that handles any category transparently).

items.description: Behavior for the three previously-hardcoded categories (food, rent, utilities) is unchanged.
items.how_to_detect: Re-run the pre-existing TestRunningTotalsByCategory tests (test_accumulates_in_posting_order, test_untouched_categories_stay_empty) and confirm they pass unmodified in behavior.

items.description: A regression test in tests/test_report.py posts to an account with a category outside the original hardcoded three and asserts running_totals_by_category returns the correct totals for it (not merely that it doesn't crash).
items.how_to_detect: Read tests/test_report.py for a new test posting to a category like "travel" and asserting on the returned series' actual values.

items.description: The full test suite and lint gate pass after the fix.
items.how_to_detect: Run `pytest tests/` and `ruff check .` from the fixture root; both must succeed with no new failures relative to the pinned baseline.
